conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I've reviewed the eligibility rules. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children under the age of 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have two children - Alex who is 12 and Blake who is 8. They both live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know you have Alex (aged 12) and Blake (aged 8) living with you.

Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's good to know.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. 

Are you responsible for Alex and Blake - meaning you're either living with them (which you've confirmed) or paying towards their upkeep such as food, clothes, or pocket money?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. They live with me and I'm responsible for all their needs - food, clothes, school things, everything.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Just a few more questions to ensure I have all the information: Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for extended periods?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in hospital or care. They're both at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question: Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year? 

(This doesn't affect your eligibility to claim Child Benefit, but it may affect whether you need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not say - is that necessary to know if I'm eligible?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's absolutely fine - you don't need to share that information with me. It doesn't affect your eligibility for Child Benefit itself, only whether there might be a tax charge to consider later, which is a separate matter.

Based on all the information you've provided, I now have enough to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit. Let me provide you with my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's the assessment for each child:

**For Alex (aged 12):**
You qualify for Child Benefit because Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their upkeep, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**For Blake (aged 8):**
You qualify for Child Benefit because Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their upkeep, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

You can claim Child Benefit for both children. Remember that only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child, but since no one else is claiming for Alex or Blake, you're free to proceed with your application.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the government website or by contacting HMRC. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 12 years old (under 16), lives in the UK with the claimant, the claimant is responsible for Alex's upkeep including food, clothes and school needs, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care or residential accommodation. According to the eligibility rules, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit as they are responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK with the child.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives in the UK with the claimant, the claimant is responsible for Blake's upkeep including food, clothes and school needs, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Blake is not in hospital, care or residential accommodation. According to the eligibility rules, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit as they are responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK with the child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in Manchester, UK with two children: Alex (aged 12) and Blake (aged 8). The claimant is responsible for both children's upkeep, neither child is in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. Both children are under 16 years old. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.
